JPM Collar, Quick macro overview - Intraday post (30/Sept)

Weekly post:

No downside confirmation - Weekly post (29/Sept - 03/Oct)

Window of Risk (FREE post):

Window of Risk, Disbalance - Investigating the story that the market tells

In the weekly post, I wrote about the JPM collar expo. Discretionary investors remain near neutral due to economic and geo risks (e.g., tariffs mostly) despite solid macro/earnings. Rates/equity correlation has turned less hostile as rates vol slides back toward pre-2022 ranges, reducing headwinds if that persists.CTAs are net long but are small buyers in flat/up tapes and asymmetric sellers on down tapes. ~93% of firms in blackout now, ~95% by week-end; window re-opens ~Oct 24. Liquidity backstops from corporates are thin near-term.NFP event risk is underpriced making it very sensitive to any surprise. VIEXEQ/VIX spread is near at YTD highs into earnings. Single-name dispersion and mega-cap event risk loom larger than index tail in the very near term. Heavy gold skew inversion also tells that investors are hedging macro risk. Positioning isn’t euphoric.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POSTCoding today’s positioning:

The speed profile is heavily negative, with a very supportive centroid of 6604.73

The downside pivot is 6611.39, from this, the heavy downside gamma would start to be magnetic, towards 6590/75 max. 6590 is a very sticky and pinning level after 1.30PM

Upside pivot is 6656.02,with upside target of 6678/80; this area looks unbreakable after 1.30PMhowever, before this, it tests the upside skew, and if vol realizes down, but call skew remains bid, we can see 6690, and even 6708 as magnet.

However the main bet is ranging between 6624 - 6678, with a magnetic 6653 level.Secondary bet is the upward stick, end reversion.Third bet is the downside vol burning,and 4th is the upward pin. Looks like another good premium collecting day. Downside is well-supplied.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
